title: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched a Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station on April 11. This mission highlights Northrop Grumman's reliance on a competitor for resupply flights to the ISS.

title: Moog Technology Keeps Artemis II Astronauts Safe During Historic Lunar Mission
summary: Moog Inc. provided critical technology for the Artemis II lunar mission, ensuring the safety of astronauts during the historic journey. The company's precision motion and fluid control systems were integral to the mission's success.

title: Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission
summary: The Artemis 2 mission, the first human mission beyond Earth orbit in over 50 years, successfully concluded with the Orion spacecraft splashing down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10. This marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: HawkEye 360 files to go public
summary: HawkEye 360, a space-based radio-frequency signals intelligence company based in Herndon, Virginia, has filed to go public. The company provides services to government agencies.

title: Spacety raises $190 million to scale satellite manufacturing, plans IPO
summary: Spacety, a Chinese satellite manufacturer, raised $190 million in equity financing to expand its vertically integrated satellite manufacturing and data services. The company also announced plans for an IPO.

title: GSOA and Novaspace Launch the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026 in Singapore
summary: GSOA and Novaspace are co-organizing the inaugural Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026 in Singapore, scheduled for May 19, 2026, at The Fullerton Hotel.

title: Commerce Department budget proposal revives concerns about TraCSS
summary: A budget proposal for the Department of Commerce has raised concerns about the future of the civil space traffic management system, TraCSS. The proposal casts doubt on the system's development and implementation.

title: China’s Chang’e-7 arrives at spaceport for lunar south pole exploration mission
summary: China's Chang'e-7 lunar spacecraft has arrived at Wenchang spaceport for launch preparations. The mission is planned for liftoff in the second half of 2026 to explore the lunar south pole.

title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return
summary: NASA is preparing for the Artemis 2 mission's return, which will involve a fiery reentry test on April 10 followed by a splashdown off the California coast.

title: PLD Space secures $35 million European Investment Bank loan for MIURA 5 launcher
summary: PLD Space secured a $35 million loan from the European Investment Bank to fund the final development of its MIURA 5 lightweight rocket. The MIURA 5 is designed to carry payloads of up to 1 ton to low Earth orbit.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast, Episode 205, covers details about the Artemis 2 mission. Hosts Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the flight progress and significance of Artemis 2.

title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
summary: SpaceX launched Northrop Grumman's second 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to the International Space Station (ISS) on April 11. This marks a significant mission to resupply ISS astronauts.

title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
summary: NASA is not stopping after the Artemis 2 moon mission success and has ambitious plans for future lunar exploration under the Artemis program. The agency is preparing for Artemis 3 and beyond to further advance human presence on the Moon.

title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on April 10, completing a historic mission around the moon that set records and paved the way for future lunar exploration.

title: NEAF 2026 returns to New York this weekend with all-star astronaut lineup and 'world's largest' space expo
summary: NEAF 2026, the world's largest space expo, returns to New York this weekend featuring an all-star astronaut lineup. The event will attract thousands of space enthusiasts, professionals, and skywatchers.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon, as shown in a newly released NASA timelapse. The footage captures the moon crossing the sun, offering a unique extended view of totality and the solar corona.

title: Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late
summary: A rare long-period comet, C/2025 R3 (Pan-STARRS), will be visible before sunrise in April after 170,000 years. The article provides key dates, viewing tips, and the best mornings to observe the comet.

title: 'They’re not shipping cows up to Mars': 'For All Mankind' creators talk scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5 (interview)
summary: 'For All Mankind' season 5 creators discuss the show's scientific accuracy and real-world inspirations for its Mars colonial narrative. The interview highlights the show's blend of fiction and real-world space industry parallels.

title: NASA took this camera gear to space aboard Artemis 2, and you can own it, too!
summary: NASA is auctioning off camera gear used aboard the Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed lunar flight in over 50 years. The equipment captured iconic images of space and is now available for public purchase.

title: Artemis 2 spies a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon | Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026
summary: A new photo from the Artemis 2 mission captures Earth as a crescent above the moon's horizon. This image highlights the mission's progress toward future lunar exploration.

title: La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords
summary: Los astronautas de la misión Artemis II de la NASA regresaron a la Tierra tras establecer récords en su vuelo de prueba a la Luna, marcando el primer viaje lunar tripulado en más de 50 años.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
summary: NASA launched the Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission to the International Space Station, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo including science and technology demonstrations. The Cygnus XL spacecraft lifted off aboard a Northrop Grumman rocket on Saturday morning.

title: Artemis II Splashes Down
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved a historic splashdown in the Pacific Ocean on April 10, 2026, after a nearly 10-day crewed spaceflight that surpassed Apollo 13's record for the farthest distance traveled. The mission also captured unprecedented views of the Moon's far side.

title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, successfully splashed down after a historic lunar flyby, marking the first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years.

title: New Perspective of Home
summary: Artemis II's lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, will capture a unique perspective of the Moon and Earth aligned in the same frame. The Moon will appear in sharp detail in the foreground with Earth softly lit and distant in the background.

title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
summary: The crew of NASA's Artemis II mission captured stunning images of Earth from the far side of the Moon. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) has introduced the WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, capable of generating arbitrary wind gradients and gusts via a Python API. A companion WindProbe is also available for flow surveys using the lab's OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
summary: Dan Florez, test director for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, describes witnessing the Artemis I launch from the Kennedy Space Center on November 16, 2022. The event marked a historic moment as NASA's Space Launch System engines ignited for the mission.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers the WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, capable of generating arbitrary wind gradients and gusts via a Python API. A companion WindProbe is also available for quick flow surveys using the lab's OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: Starstruck
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a photo of the Milky Way galaxy on April 7, 2026, showcasing its spiral structure with two arms extending from a central bar of stars. The galaxy spans over 100,000 light-years, with Earth located on one of its spiral arms.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and announcing future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at its Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing efforts to enhance launch infrastructure at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II conducted a lunar flyby, setting a record with the Orion spacecraft carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the ISS, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches coincide with the halfway point of NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is set to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website has reported on recent developments in the space industry, though specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at ESA's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improvements in attitude control systems. This innovation is crucial for precise navigation and mission success in space exploration.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study found that lasers could effectively propel the aerogels in microgravity, with potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This innovation could enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful reconnection, marking a positive turn for the mission.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo announced a new Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. This development highlights the company's focus on advancing satellite technology for higher power capabilities in VLEO.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog has supplied over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company highlights its extensive contributions to the upcoming lunar mission.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: The Artemis II mission features the European Service Module (ESM) built by Airbus Defence and Space, offering hope for European involvement in spaceflight. This collaboration highlights ESA's contribution to NASA's lunar program.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has secured $50 million in a Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investments from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of Launch highlights the competitive pressures in the space industry, noting that deadlines and capability gates can determine winners and losers in launch services. The 'State of Launch 2026' report emphasizes the constraints that shape eligibility and competition.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. The announcement highlights the company's focus on advancing its satellite technology.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. It highlights the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM as a key trend in the space industry.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-made radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. The technology will serve as an early warning system for potential solar radiation threats.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: The Trump administration proposed a $18.8 billion NASA budget with significant cuts to science and STEM missions, prompting advocates and lawmakers to resist the reductions. This marks a recurring conflict over NASA's funding priorities.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with Activate Capital and Shield Capital co-leading the investment.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet in November 2023. An executive explained the airline's decision to transition to Starlink's connectivity services.

title: Amazon CEO Targets Mid-2026 Launch for Amazon Leo Service
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced in a shareholder letter that Amazon's satellite constellation service, Amazon Leo, is planned to launch in mid-2026. No specific launch date was provided.

title: BryceTech Report Shows SpaceX Accounted for 50% of Launches in 2025
summary: SpaceX accounted for 50% of all launches in 2025, according to a BryceTech report. The report highlights a shift in the space industry toward small satellites.

title: EnduroSat and Shield Space Strike European Partnership for Defense Missions
summary: EnduroSat and Shield Space have formed a strategic partnership to jointly operate space defense missions in Europe. The collaboration aims to leverage their combined technologies for on-orbit operations.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to push data from centralized nodes to the tactical edge for faster decision-making in conflict scenarios. Network integration remains a key challenge in this effort.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor launched two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisit rates. This innovation aims to enhance commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in contracts for space domain awareness under the 'Andromeda' initiative. The contracts, managed by Space Systems Command, focus on geosynchronous reconnaissance.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG) appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its second CEO, replacing Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to accelerate the development of maneuverable spacecraft and expand its manufacturing capacity. The startup is transitioning into full production mode.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. Eiichi Yonekura, the company's President & CEO, discussed these plans in a recent interview.
